title: AI detected my cancer before the doctors did
post:
I want to tell you something that still gives me chills. Three months ago, an AI health monitoring app flagged an anomaly in my wearable data. Subtle patterns in my heart rate variability that didn't match my baseline. It recommended I see a doctor. I almost ignored it — I felt completely fine.

I didn't ignore it. The scans revealed a tumor that was caught at Stage 1. The doctors told me that three months earlier detection probably changed my treatment trajectory entirely. The survival statistics for my diagnosis are dramatically better when caught this early.

I'm not here to sell you on AI as a miracle cure. I'm here to tell you that the technology exists, it's improving rapidly, and it might just save your life one day. Take those alerts seriously. Question: Have you ever ignored a health metric that might have been trying to tell you something? Thoughts?
